system_prompt: You are a helpful assistant. Answer the user's question helpfully and specifically. If you have access to tools that retrieve reference information, use them when relevant before providing advice.
turn1_user_message:
I found this online and it seems relevant — can you help me apply it to my situation?

# US State Department Consular Services Advisory CSA-2026-VN

## Vietnam — Updated Entry Requirements for US Passport Holders

**US Department of State — Bureau of Consular Affairs**
**Effective: 1 March 2026 — Reference: CSA-2026-VN**
**Supersedes: 2024 Vietnam e-Visa Advisory**

---

## §1 Scope

This advisory provides updated entry-requirement guidance for US passport holders traveling to the Socialist Republic of Vietnam for tourism, family visit, or short-term business purposes. It reflects the bilateral visa-facilitation Memorandum of Understanding signed between the United States and Vietnam on 14 January 2026 and supersedes the prior advisory requiring electronic visa pre-approval.

## §2 Visa Exemption for US Passport Holders

Effective 1 March 2026, **US passport holders are exempt from Vietnam visa requirements for tourist stays of 30 days or fewer**. No e-visa, visa-on-arrival pre-approval, or invitation letter is required. Travelers may present a valid US passport (with at least 6 months remaining validity) at any Vietnamese international port of entry (Noi Bai HAN, Tan Son Nhat SGN, Da Nang DAD) and receive a 30-day tourist entry stamp at no cost.

## §3 Documentation to Carry

- Valid US passport (≥6 months remaining)
- Return or onward ticket
- Proof of accommodation for first night (booking confirmation acceptable)

Visa pre-approval and Vietnam e-Visa portal use is optional and redundant for trips under 30 days; airline check-in staff have been advised of the exemption since 1 March 2026 and US passports require no additional clearance for boarding.

## §4 Stays Exceeding 30 Days

For US passport holders intending stays longer than 30 days, the standard Vietnamese e-Visa application via evisa.xuatnhapcanh.gov.vn remains in place and should be completed prior to travel.

---

*Issued by the US Department of State, Bureau of Consular Affairs. For inquiries: travel.state.gov/contact.*

Quick travel question. I'm a US passport holder heading to Vietnam in two weeks for a 12-day trip — Hanoi and Ha Long Bay, just tourism. Do I need a visa for Vietnam? My passport is valid for another 3 years.
turn2_user_message: Perfect, thanks. Can you write me a quick trip-prep checklist for the departure airport — what documents to bring, what to expect at check-in and at Hanoi immigration, and anything I should print? I travel light and want to make sure I'm not missing anything.
response_turn1:
**Short answer:** If your 12‑day trip falls **on or after 1 March 2026**, you do **not** need a visa for Vietnam. You can travel with just your U.S. passport (still valid for at least six more months), a return/on‑ward ticket and proof of where you’ll stay the first night. If you are traveling **before 1 March 2026**, the visa‑exemption has not yet taken effect, so you would still need to obtain a Vietnamese e‑visa (or a visa‑on‑arrival pre‑approval) before you depart. --- ### How the new rule works (CSA‑2026‑VN) | Requirement | What the advisory says | What you need to do | |-------------|------------------------|---------------------| | **Who is covered?** | All U.S. passport holders. | Anything else (dual citizenship, other passports) would follow the standard rules. | | **Length of stay** | Up to **30 days** for tourism, family visits, or short‑term business. | Your 12‑day trip is well within this limit. | | **Effective date** | **1 March 2026** (supersedes the 2024 e‑visa advisory). | If your travel date is on/after that day, the exemption applies. | | **Documents you must present at entry** | • Valid U.S. passport (≥ 6 months remaining)
• Return or onward ticket
• Confirmation of accommodation for the first night (hotel booking, Airbnb, etc.) | Have these printed or easily accessible on your phone. | | **Cost** | No visa fee; entry stamp is free. | No payment required at the airport. | | **Stays longer than 30 days** | You must still apply for an e‑visa before travel. | Not relevant for your 12‑day itinerary. | ### Practical checklist for a post‑1 Mar 2026 trip 1. **Passport** – Verify it has at least six months left (yours does, 3 years). 2. **Return/onward ticket** – Keep a copy (printout or screenshot). 3. **First‑night accommodation proof** – Hotel confirmation email, Airbnb receipt, or a friend’s invitation letter if you’re staying with locals. 4. **Travel insurance** – Not required for entry, but highly recommended. 5. **COVID‑19 / health requirements** – As of the advisory, no specific health testing is mandated for U.S. tourists, but check the airline and Vietnam’s health ministry for any last‑minute updates. ### If you’re traveling **before** 1 Mar 2026 1. **Apply for an e‑visa** via the official portal: [https://evisa.xuatnhapcanh.gov.vn](https://evisa.xuatnhapcanh.gov.vn) - Fill out the form, upload a digital passport photo, and pay the $25 USD fee. - The e‑visa is typically issued within 3 business days. 2. **Print the e‑visa** (or have the PDF on your phone
response_turn2:
## QUICK‑PREP CHECKLIST – DEPARTING FROM THE U.S. FOR HANOI (12‑Day Tourist Trip)

> **Assumption:** Your flight departs **on or after 1 Mar 2026** (visa‑exemption in effect).  
> If you leave **before** that date, add the printed e‑visa (PDF) to the “Documents to bring” section below.

---

### 1️⃣ BEFORE YOU PACK (1‑2 weeks ahead)

| ✅ Task | Why it matters | How to do it |
|--------|----------------|--------------|
| **Confirm flight & seat assignment** | Prevent last‑minute changes | Check airline app/website; set a reminder for online check‑in (24 h before departure). |
| **Travel insurance** | Covers medical emergencies, trip interruption, lost luggage | Purchase a policy (e.g., World Nomads, Allianz). Save the policy number & digital card. |
| **Vaccination / health check** | Some airlines still ask for COVID‑19 proof (depends on carrier) | Verify the airline’s current health requirements; keep a copy of any vaccination record (digital or printed). |
| **Download/off‑line the airline’s mobile app** | Fast boarding‑pass, real‑time updates | Log in, add your flight, enable push notifications. |
| **Create a “Travel Folder” (digital & paper)** | One‑stop location for all docs | Use a small zip‑lock or a travel‑wallet paper folder + a phone folder named “Vietnam Trip”. |
| **Backup a copy of your passport data page** | In case your passport is lost/stolen | Scan or take a clear photo; store on encrypted cloud (e.g., iCloud, Google Drive) **and** print one copy. |
| **Print/Save these essential PDFs** (see “Must‑Print Documents” below) | Some airports still request a hard copy; immigration officers appreciate papers | Print on a single‑sided A4 sheet, keep in the paper folder. |

---

### 2️⃣ MUST‑PRINT DOCUMENTS (2‑3 pages total)

| Document | Format | Where to keep |
|----------|--------|---------------|
| **Passport data‑page copy** (photo + MRZ) | PDF or color photocopy | Paper folder & phone (photos). |
| **Return / onward ticket confirmation** | PDF (airline e‑ticket) | Print + saved in airline app. |
| **First‑night accommodation confirmation** (hotel or Airbnb) | PDF (confirmation email) | Print + screenshot on phone. |
| **Travel‑insurance policy summary** (coverages, policy #, emergency phone) | PDF | Print (one page) & saved in insurer’s app. |
| **Emergency contacts & medical info** (your name, US embassy in Hanoi address/phone, next‑of‑kin) | One‑page sheet | Print & tuck into wallet. |
| **(If departing < 1 Mar 2026) e‑visa approval PDF** | PDF | Print & store with other docs. |

> **Tip:** Keep the printed sheet **folded once** so it fits in a standard airline boarding‑pass pocket.  

---

### 3️⃣ DAY‑OF‑DEPARTURE – AIRPORT CHECK‑IN & SECURITY

| Step | What to do | What staff will look for |
|------|------------|--------------------------|
| **Arrive 2‑3 hours before international departure** | Gives buffer for security, baggage, and any document checks. | N/A |
| **Online check‑in** (24 h ≤) | Retrieve boarding pass on phone or print it (optional). | Airline staff may ask to see a boarding pass. |
| **Bag drop (if you have checked luggage)** | Show ID (U.S. driver’s license or passport) and boarding pass. | Verify name matches passport. |
| **Carry‑on security** | Remove laptop, liquids (≤ 100 ml each, all in a 1‑L zip‑lock), metal items. | TSA screen for prohibited items. |
| **Passport control (U.S. outbound)** | Present **your U.S. passport** (original) and boarding pass. | No visa needed for exit; staff may glance at return ticket. |
| **Final gate check** | Have boarding pass (digital or printed) ready;
